change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value: 1.     Policy analysis- gaps, opportunities and assess enforcement of the prevalent policies on DRR/ Flood Resilience2.     Expenditure tracking of municipalities in CCA/DRR-Research3.     Collect qualitative information in the FRMC endline to understand communities' perceptions of their access to government-led activities and resources that strengthen their resilience to floods and other climate-related hazards.4.     Landscape assessment of disaster and climate vulnerability of two river watersheds in Sudurpaschim Province5.     Conduct Capacity Building -Training/Refresher on budget allocation on flood resilience including DRR/CCA-6.     Conduct province-level stakeholder meetings to share the policy analysis and discuss the opportunities to include climate-hazard resilience in local/provincial DRM Plans, policies and strategies7.     Sharing Meeting at CBDRM Platform, NDRRMA and related stakeholders8.     Support continuation/activation of monitoring & evaluation committees of local governments9.     Sensitization Workshop/Discussion with private sector associations (FNCCI, CNI and foundations) on the potential of private sector engagement in disaster risk reduction, climate adaptation10.  Consultancy/Research on CCA and DRR, IBI, EWS, SRSP- Response11.  CDMC Follow up and Capacity Building in Rangun River Watershed12.  Research, Advocacy on Climate Change Adaptation, Disaster Risk Reduction and Flood Resilience13.  Develop/Update/revise DRM plans and LDCRP14.  Provide design and implementation model of activities by CDMCs and local governments15.  Develop knowledge product, including findings and recommendations of budget allocation, and Municipal DRR/CCA governance mechanism16.  Consolidate high impact cases on flood resilience and integration in development plans to advocate with the provincial and federal government to influence models/guidelines for local governments.17.  Community Intervention for Disaster Mitigation18.  Development of a multi-stakeholder platform for enhancing resilience to flood /disasters and climate change19.  Develop knowledge products on learnings from FRMC process, landscape approach and share with local, and sub-national governments20.  Province Level Coordination Meetings21.  Program Inception, MPAC Meetings, Stakeholder Monitoring22.  Baseline23.  Post Flood Studies24.  Training - Capacity Building - Advocacy Events for CDMCs25.  End line26.  Province Level Sharing and Review Workshop27.  National Level Sharing Workshop28.  Media Engagement

snapshot.executing_agency: Ministry of Federal Affairs & General Administration, Ministry of Forests and Environment
